chapters.chapter_summary: Introduction to surgical tools such as needle holders, scissors, and forceps. Demonstration of interrupted suturing techniques.
Demonstration of various suturing techniques including interrupted sutures, continuous sutures, and mattress sutures. Use of different types of scissors and forceps to manipulate suture material.

Demonstration of different suturing techniques such as vertical mattress sutures, deep dermal sutures, horizontal mattress sutures, and subcuticular suturing. Techniques for tying knots and securing sutures are shown.

Demonstration of locking mattress sutures, interrupted sutures, and running sutures. Techniques for tying knots and securing sutures are continued. Emphasis on maintaining sterility and proper disposal of contaminated items.

operative_note.title: Surgical Instrument Handling and Suturing Techniques

SOAP.Subjective: I performed demonstrations of surgical instrument handling and various suturing techniques on a practice pad.
SOAP.Objective: I identified the proper handling of surgical instruments such as needle holders, scissors, and forceps. I observed the demonstration of interrupted suturing, continuous suturing, horizontal mattress suturing, vertical mattress suturing, deep dermal suturing, and Aberdeen knot suturing. I noted the use of different colored suture materials including black, purple, and white.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the demonstrations as accurate and detailed, with a focus on proper technique and the importance of maintaining tension and precision during suturing. Specific techniques such as tying surgeon's knots and reef knots were demonstrated, as well as the importance of trimming excess suture material.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the demonstration by trimming the excess suture material and ensuring all knots were secure. I emphasized the need for consistent tension and the importance of maintaining sterility throughout the procedure.
